results.possibleIdentity: Mitchell David Waite (born May 3, 1946) is a noted American author, publisher, and computer programmer, best known as the founder of The Waite Group, a major publisher of computer books. Between 1968 and 1971, he was active at the College of Marin in Kentfield, California, where he studied physical sciences and mathematics before continuing his studies in physics at Sonoma State University. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, Waite was deeply involved in the intersection of electronics, physics, and art, specifically focusing on technical projects involving light, lasers, strobes, and DIY electronic sensors. These interests culminated in his first book, 'Projects in Sight, Sound and Sensation' (1974), which provided instructions for building electronic art devices such as laser light shows, perception stimulators (strobe-based art), and neon lamp displays. This early work aligns with the goals of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit that promoted collaborations between artists and engineers during that era. His technical expertise also extended to the early personal computing movement, where he was an early member of the Homebrew Computer Club and became a prominent figure in the San Francisco Bay Area's technical community, particularly in locations like Corte Madera, Kentfield, and San Rafael.
